items.title: USDA final rule tightens SNAP retailer stocking standards (compliance by Nov. 4, 2026)
items.summary: USDA Food and Nutrition Service published a final rule updating SNAP retailer stocking standards, requiring most SNAP-authorized retailers to carry at least seven varieties in each of four staple food categories (dairy, fruits/vegetables, grains, protein). Retailers must also offer a perishable variety in at least three of the four staple categories, and certain items (like jerky and snack bars) can no longer count toward staple food requirements; all SNAP-authorized retailers must comply by Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP funding for certain noncitizens (effective Oct. 1, 2026)
items.summary: CMS released operational guidance to implement statutory changes that limit the ability for states to claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals or who fall into certain noncitizen categories identified in statute. CMS says the change begins Oct. 1, 2026 and the guidance addresses eligibility systems, applications, verification, and claims processing, while noting continued availability of federal matching funds for treatment of emergency medical conditions.

items.title: HUD issues PIH Notice 2026-12 on Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher funding, with key 2026 deadlines for PHAs
items.summary: HUD issued PIH Notice 2026-12 implementing FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) funding provisions, listing total available CY 2026 appropriations of $38,438,557,000 (including $34,557,000,000 for HAP renewal funding and $2,835,935,000 for administrative fees). The notice also lists major HAP set-aside application deadlines for PHAs, including June 26, 2026 (5pm local time) for several categories and Sept. 25, 2026 (5pm local time) for the HUD-VASH category.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: WIC: USDA sets FY 2026 fruit/vegetable cash value benefit amounts (11.8% inflation adjustment from FY 2022 baseline)
items.summary: USDA FNS issued WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 stating the FY 2026 cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline. The FY 2026 monthly CVV/B amounts are $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or partially breastfeeding participants.

items.title: SSA: 2026 Social Security cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) is 2.8%
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% COLA for 2026. SSA’s fact sheet shows the average monthly benefit for all retired workers rising from $2,015 to $2,071 and the average for all disabled workers rising from $1,586 to $1,630.

items.title: FAFSA deadline reminder: June 30, 2026 is the federal deadline for 2025–26 aid year
items.summary: USAGov lists June 30, 2026 as the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2025–26 school year (and June 30, 2027 for 2026–27). Students also need an FSA ID to sign and submit the FAFSA, and USAGov notes it can take up to three days for SSA to confirm an FSA ID for uses beyond signing and submitting the first FAFSA.

items.title: IRS updates Child Tax Credit page (up to $2,200 per child; EITC/ACTC refunds not issued before mid-February)
items.summary: The IRS Child Tax Credit page lists a maximum Child Tax Credit of up to $2,200 per qualifying child and an Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) of up to $1,700 per qualifying child (depending on income), with at least $2,500 in earned income required for ACTC eligibility. The IRS also reiterates that if you claim EITC or ACTC, it cannot issue refunds before mid-February and the delay applies to the entire refund.

items.title: IRS posts EITC maximum credit amounts and investment income limit for tax year 2025
items.summary: IRS EITC tables for tax year 2025 list maximum credits of $649 (no qualifying children), $4,328 (one child), $7,152 (two children), and $8,046 (three or more children). The same table lists an investment income limit of $11,950 or less for EITC eligibility.
